lesson.title: Data Cleaning
lesson.body: Real data is messy. Key cleaning operations: df.isnull() detects missing values, df.dropna() removes them, df.fillna(value) fills them. df.duplicated() and df.drop_duplicates() handle duplicate rows. String operations use df['col'].str.method(). Type conversion: pd.to_datetime(), pd.to_numeric(). Apply custom functions with .apply() (row/column) or .map() (element-wise on Series).

rooms.question: Complete the code to fill missing values with the column mean:
rooms.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.DataFrame({'score': [85, np.nan, 90, np.nan, 75]})
df['score'] = df['score'].___( df['score'].mean())
rooms.explanation: Series.fillna(value) replaces NaN with the specified value. Common strategy: fill with mean (numeric) or mode (categorical).

rooms.question: Complete the code to remove rows with any missing value:
rooms.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.DataFrame({'a':[1,np.nan,3],'b':[4,5,np.nan]})
cleaned = df.___()
rooms.explanation: df.dropna() removes rows where ANY column has NaN (default). Use dropna(subset=['col']) to only check specific columns, or dropna(how='all') to only drop if ALL are NaN.

rooms.question: What does df.isnull().sum() return?
answers: The count of missing values per column
Total number of NaN values in the DataFrame
Rows with at least one NaN
rooms.explanation: df.isnull() returns a boolean DataFrame. .sum() adds up True values (treating True=1) per column, giving the count of NaN per column.

rooms.question: Complete the code to convert a string column to datetime:
rooms.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'date': ['2023-01-15', '2023-02-20']})
df['date'] = pd.___( df['date'])
rooms.explanation: pd.to_datetime(series) converts string representations of dates to pandas datetime64 dtype, enabling .dt accessor for date components.

rooms.question: Order the standard data cleaning pipeline:
rooms.explanation: Standard cleaning order: audit → handle missing → fix types → dedup. Always start with understanding what's wrong before fixing it.

boss.name: The NaN Specter
boss.taunt: I hide in your data. Averages computed with me are lies. Ignore me at your peril.

questions.question: Complete the code to strip whitespace from a string column:
questions.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'name': ['  Alice  ', ' Bob', 'Carol ']})
df['name'] = df['name'].str.___()
questions.explanation: Series.str.strip() removes leading and trailing whitespace. .str accessor provides vectorized string methods for the entire Series.

questions.question: df.apply(func, axis=1) applies func to:
answers: Each row (axis=1 means apply along columns for each row)
questions.explanation: axis=1 means the function receives each row as a Series. axis=0 (default) applies to each column as a Series.

questions.question: Complete the code to convert 'price' column to numeric, coercing errors to NaN:
questions.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'price': ['10.5', '20', 'N/A', '15.0']})
df['price'] = pd.to_numeric(df['price'], errors='___')
questions.explanation: errors='coerce' converts non-numeric values to NaN instead of raising an error. errors='raise' (default) raises ValueError. errors='ignore' returns the original value.
